cli-basics.value: `claude` starts an interactive session by default; `-p`/`--print` runs non-interactively (useful for pipes).

search-refs.value: In-REPL: ask codebase questions in natural language; reference files/dirs with @-mentions (e.g. `@app/main.py`).

permission-modes.value: acceptEdits, auto, bypassPermissions, default, dontAsk, plan (`--permission-mode <mode>`)
permission-modes.notes: Verified set is broader than the design's illustrative example — confirms the value of verifying vs memory.

secrets.value: API-key auth via the ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var (or apiKeyHelper); keep secrets out of prompts, CLAUDE.md, and committed files.
secrets.provenance: claude --help (--bare: 'Anthropic auth is strictly ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or apiKeyHelper')
secrets.notes: Hygiene guidance is principle-level; the ANTHROPIC_API_KEY/apiKeyHelper mechanism is the verified anchor.

untrusted-content.value: A workspace-trust dialog gates untrusted directories (skipped in non-interactive `-p` mode — only run -p in trusted dirs).
untrusted-content.notes: Prompt-injection defense is principle-level; the trust dialog is the verified control surface.

checkpoint-rewind.value: In-REPL `/rewind` checkpoints/restores recent changes to undo them; `--no-session-persistence` disables saving sessions.

memory.value: Project memory via CLAUDE.md, auto-discovered at startup (auto-memory).

context-cmds.value: In-REPL slash commands: /context, /compact, /clear (inspect and manage the context window).

plan-mode.value: Plan mode (`--permission-mode plan`): Claude plans without writing changes until you approve.

thinking.value: Effort levels low, medium, high, xhigh, max (`--effort <level>`).

test-run.value: Tests run through the Bash tool (e.g. `pytest`); no dedicated CLI flag — Claude invokes the project's existing test runner.
test-run.provenance: no dedicated CLI surface in `claude --help`; mechanism is tool-use (conceptual, version-independent)
test-run.notes: Conceptual; the project's runner is pytest for taskflow-api.

git-pr.value: `--from-pr [value]` resumes a session linked to a PR by number/URL; PR creation uses the `gh` CLI via the Bash tool.
git-pr.provenance: claude --help (--from-pr); gh is an external tool

review-cmds.value: `claude ultrareview` runs a cloud multi-agent review of the branch/PR; /code-review and /security-review are in-REPL skills.
review-cmds.provenance: claude --help (ultrareview command); /code-review and /security-review confirmed present in in-REPL `/help` (2026-05-30)

custom-commands.value: Custom slash commands are user-defined skills, invoked as /name; `--disable-slash-commands` turns all skills off.
custom-commands.notes: The on-disk location (e.g. .claude/commands/) is a filesystem convention — verify the exact path via docs before hardcoding.

skills.value: Skills resolve via /skill-name; reusable packaged capabilities.

subagents.value: `--agent <agent>` / `--agents <json>` set session agents; the `agents` subcommand manages background agents.
subagents.provenance: claude --help (--agent, --agents, `agents` command); on-disk file schema from official docs code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents (2026-05-31)
subagents.notes: The inline `--agents <json>` form (with a per-agent `description`) is --help-verified. On-disk subagents are Markdown files at `.claude/agents/<name>.md` (project scope, checked into VCS) with YAML front matter — docs-confirmed (2026-05-31): only `name` and `description` are required; `tools` is a comma-separated allowlist (e.g. `Read, Grep, Glob`) that INHERITS ALL TOOLS IF OMITTED (so read-only fencing must list tools explicitly); `model` accepts `sonnet`/`opus`/`haiku`/a full model ID/`inherit` (default `inherit`); the Markdown body IS the agent's entire system prompt (it does not receive the main Claude Code system prompt). Other optional fields: disallowedTools, permissionMode, mcpServers, hooks, maxTurns, skills, initialPrompt, memory, effort, background, isolation, color.

hooks.value: Hooks run a command on a lifecycle event, configured in settings.json as `hooks.<Event>: [{matcher, hooks:[{type:"command", command}]}]`. Common events: PreToolUse (can block), PostToolUse, Stop, SessionStart, PreCompact. `--include-hook-events` streams them; `--bare` skips hooks.
hooks.provenance: claude --help (--include-hook-events; --bare 'skip hooks'); settings.json schema (hooks event enum + {matcher, hooks:[...]} structure, via update-config skill, 2026-05-30)
hooks.notes: Event-name enum verified against the settings.json schema @ 2.1.158 (~30 events). Teach the common ones (PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop/SessionStart/PreCompact); defer the full, growing enum to the docs rather than hardcoding it.

mcp.value: `claude mcp add <name> -- <cmd>` adds a local stdio server (subprocess); `--transport http <url>` adds a remote one. `claude mcp get`/`list` health-check connections; a project `.mcp.json` server shows as `⏸ Pending approval` until approved. `--mcp-config <files>` loads per-session; `--strict-mcp-config` ignores others.
mcp.provenance: claude mcp --help (add/add-json/get/list/remove/serve); verified live 2026-05-30 — a stdlib stdio server registered via `mcp add` reported `✓ Connected` via `mcp get`; `.mcp.json` format captured from `mcp add -s project`.

plugins.value: `claude plugin|plugins` manages plugins; `--plugin-dir <path>` / `--plugin-url <url>` load a plugin for one session.

headless.value: `-p`/`--print` runs headlessly; `--output-format text|json|stream-json` shapes the output.

ci.value: CI runs use headless `-p` with `--output-format json` (and optionally `--max-budget-usd`); a GitHub Action wraps this.
ci.provenance: claude --help confirms -p, --output-format, --max-budget-usd; the GitHub Action is an external integration
ci.notes: CLI mechanism verified (-p/--output-format/--max-budget-usd). The GitHub Action wrapper stays unverified: confirm its name/setup against official docs in an environment with GitHub Actions (maintainer's env uses Gitea, no GitHub Action available to verify, 2026-05-30).

worktrees.value: `-w`/`--worktree [name]` creates a git worktree for the session; `--tmux` opens it in a tmux session.

settings.value: `--settings <file-or-json>` loads settings; `--setting-sources user,project,local` selects which sources load.
settings.notes: The default settings.json file locations are a filesystem convention — verify exact paths via docs before hardcoding.

ide.value: `--ide` auto-connects to an IDE on startup when exactly one valid IDE is available.

output-styles.value: Output styles customize Claude's response behavior: the Default (standard SWE system prompt) plus three built-in styles — Proactive (acts immediately, prefers action over planning), Explanatory (adds educational 'Insights'), and Learning (collaborative; adds TODO(human) markers for you to implement). Select via `/config` -> Output style (saved to `.claude/settings.local.json`), or set the `outputStyle` settings field directly.
output-styles.provenance: official docs (built-in output styles) + user-confirmed via `/config` @ 2.1.158, 2026-05-30
output-styles.notes: The standalone `/output-style` command was deprecated in v2.1.73 and removed in v2.1.91 — use `/config` or the `outputStyle` setting.

managed-settings.value: Enterprise/managed settings are an additional setting source layered above user/project/local.
managed-settings.provenance: claude --help (--setting-sources lists user, project, local); the managed/enterprise policy path is not shown
managed-settings.notes: Conceptual only (assumption A4). Stays unverified: the managed/enterprise settings path/precedence needs an enterprise account or official enterprise docs to confirm (not available to the maintainer, 2026-05-30).
